turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
The main role of a business analyst can vary depending on the industry, company, and specific job responsibilities. However, in general, the main role of a business analyst is to analyze the existing business processes, identify areas for improvement, and recommend solutions that align with the overall goals of the organization. 

Some of the typical tasks performed by a business analyst may include:

1. Conducting research and gathering data to inform business decisions
2. Analyzing business processes, workflows, and systems to identify inefficiencies and areas for improvement
3. Developing business requirements documentation and specifications
4. Collaborating with stakeholders to develop solutions and make decisions
5. Developing or improving business models and strategies 
6. Providing project management support and ensuring that projects are completed on time and within budget 
7. Monitoring and reporting on key performance indicators 
8. Providing training and support to end-users and other stakeholders 

Overall, a business analyst plays a key role in helping organizations to operate more efficiently and effectively, by identifying opportunities for improvement and implementing solutions that align with the company's goals and objectives.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: prière de donner d'ample details à ce sujet: Les actions/titres financières pour une société est de trois types:-1 titre de participation, -2 titre à caractère obligation durable , 3- titre à caractère obligation non durable . quelle est la différence entre les trois types de titres/actions? Aussi que veut dire un inventaire pour une société??

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Les trois types de titres financiers que peut émettre une société sont les suivants :

1. Titre de participation : Il s'agit d'une action qui donne à son propriétaire le droit de participer aux bénéfices de l'entreprise, proportionnellement au nombre d'actions détenues. Les porteurs de ces actions peuvent également prendre part aux décisions de l'entreprise, notamment lors des assemblées générales.

2. Titre à caractère obligataire durable : Ces titres sont émis dans le but de récolter des fonds auprès des investisseurs. Contrairement aux actions, ils représentent une forme de dette pour l'entreprise. Les porteurs de ces titres ont droit à un versement régulier d'intérêts, mais ne peuvent pas prétendre aux bénéfices de l'entreprise.

3. Titre à caractère obligataire non durable : Ce type de titre est également émis sous forme de dette mais pour une durée limitée. Les porteurs de ces titres ont droit à un remboursement du capital et d'un versement régulier d'intérêts jusqu'à l'échéance du titre. Après cela, la dette est entièrement remboursée et l'investisseur ne dispose plus d'aucun droit sur l'entreprise.

En ce qui concerne l'inventaire pour une société, il s'agit d'une liste détaillée de tous les biens matériels et immatériels appartenant à l'entreprise. Cela peut inclure des stocks, des équipements, des propriétés, des bons de commande, des créances et des dettes, entre autres. L'inventaire est une pièce essentielle de la comptabilité d'une entreprise car elle permet de suivre avec précision les mouvements et les flux de capitaux, et d'assurer une gestion optimale des ressources.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Please provide me a cover letter conform with this role:Job Title: CONTINUING AIRWORTHINESS MANAGER (CAM)

Job Code: HR section 

Reports To (title): Accountable Manager

SUMMARY OF THE JOB 

Reporting “firm-line” to the Accountable Manager, the incumbent will facilitate with the development,  implementation and maintenance of CAMO, systems and plans to meet GCAA objectives of being best-in-class and in compliance with all applicable regulatory & customer requirements. Additional responsibilities  to be full filled by the CAM established by the regulatory requirements as per CAR M from time to time by  GCAA/EASA or any other authority as applicable shall be automatically included in the JD and is applicable  without the need of any further specification



ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:

• Forecasting planned maintenance work 

• Reliability assessment 

• Establishing maintenance contract(s) with CAR 145 organizations to ensure  airworthiness of the aircraft as required by CAR M. 

• Ensuring the maintenance provider can begin and finish works within the planned  down-time 

• Airworthiness Directive review and embodiment 

• Service Bulletin evaluation embodiment,  

• Test flights, coordinating   

• Coordinate with GCAA during aircraft Deregistration process.  

2. The Technical Records Department, which looks after all the Aircraft Records

3. The Engineering Department, which performs analysis of in Service information, and plans modifications / repairs; 

4. The Reliability Department, which maintains oversight of the effectiveness of the  maintenance product. 

Detailed Work Planning and Scheduling 

• Budgetary Control and other considerations 

• Inventory control 

• Subcontract Co-ordination 

• Planning of safety-critical tasks 

• Co-ordination with Production Planning 

• Availability and the control of approved data 

• Considering Airworthiness Reviews 

• Ensure the maintenance of aircraft and equipment 

• Monitor the performance of products in service. 

• Recording service difficulties (Mandatory Reporting Obligations) to assess  significance.

• Timely response is required where airworthiness is affected, which means devising  and provisioning rectification action. 

• Participate in all Safety/Quality Meetings 

• Be the focal point for CAMO audits by authorities  

• mitigating potential aviation safety risks already identified by the operator; • mitigating potential aviation safety risks not yet apparent to the operator. 

• reliability improvement of the aircraft and components; and 

• improvement of the effectiveness of the AMP.

He should ensure the following: 

• The airworthiness certificate remains valid, and; 

• The FJR Private Flight aircraft is maintained in an airworthy condition, and; • Responsible for the continuing airworthiness of the aircraft operated by FJR Private  Flight.

• Any operational and emergency equipment fitted is correctly installed and  serviceable or clearly identified as unserviceable. 

• The maintenance of the aircraft is performed in accordance with the approved  maintenance programme as specified in CAR M.A.302 

• Get the AMP and its amendments approved by the Airworthiness Department of  GCAA. 

• Analysis of the applicability and effectiveness of the AMP. 

• Maintain current and updated continuing airworthiness records of aircraft. • Amendment and control of CAME. 

• Respond to non-compliance with continuing airworthiness requirements which arise  from independent organizational reviews and regulatory authorities. 

• Correspondence with GCAA in all matters relating to Continuing Airworthiness  Management system procedures and documentation. 

• Notify the Accountable Manager whenever deficiencies emerge which require his  attention in respect of finance, resources or the acceptability of airworthiness  standards. 

• An elimination of an identified hazard by the embodiment of a modification, or • reducing the safety risk.

• reducing the likelihood of the consequences of an identified hazard by performing  repetitive inspections. 

▪ Work, under the direction and guidance of the Management group, on Global,  Regional and/or local improvement projects, including: 

▪ Facilitate the training for local staff on global tools / process implementations.



KEY PROCESSES 

Ensuring the implementation CAMO activities undertaken at the organization

Contributing to the development & improvement of global & regional, as well as local, CAMO strategies and  plans



KEY PERFORMANCE INDICATOR (KPI) 

Successful implementation of CAMO System

Ensure all staff are given the required briefing and maintain a record

All audits/inspections findings are responded and closed timely in coordination with QM.

Opportunity for improvement (OFI) to be raised.



QUALIFICATIONS & SKILLS 

Knowledge, skill, and/or ability required 

• Strong inter-personal, communication and presentation skills;  

• Works effectively in a team; 

• Strong planning skills; 

• Integrity and Trust; 

• Self-motivated and takes initiative to perform in order to meet deadlines; and 

• Strong character with good negotiation skills. 

• Advanced IT skills in MS Office. Excel and other latest Manual development software available.

EDUCATION & EXPERIENCE REQUIRED

• Comprehensive knowledge of CAR M and any associated requirements and procedures; • 15 years aviation experience of which at least 8 years should be in Aviation industry in CAMO. • 5 year’s CAMO Experience on Falcon 8X or on similar aircraft; 

• At last 3 year experience as Authority Post Holder for CAMO and as ARC staff 

• A relevant engineering degree acceptable to GCAA. ‘Relevant engineering degree’ means an  engineering degree from aeronautical, mechanical, electrical, electronic, avionic.

• Knowledge of the relevant type(s) of aircraft or components maintained; this knowledge may be  demonstrated by documented evidence. 

• Training courses should be as a minimum at a level equivalent to CAR-66, Appendix III Level 1 General  Familiarization, and could be imparted by a Part/CAR-147 organization, by the manufacturer, or by  any other organization accepted by the GCAA; 

• Knowledge of maintenance standards and hold an Aircraft Maintenance Engineer’s License (AMEL) in the relevant category without limitations.



COMPLEXITY AND CREATIVITY – PROBLEM SOLVING 

• Excellent problem solving ability; and 

• Excellent long and short term strategic planning skills.



JUDGEMENTS AND DECISIONS 

• Scope to deal with complex problems unsupervised; and 

• Other corporate wide complex problems will be referred in collaboration to a higher level.



GEOGRAPHICAL COVERAGE 

Based at the Operational base and when needed, at other locations.



CONTACTS AND COMMUNICATIONS 

• Significant internal client and external customer contact 

• Significant contact with a number of National Aviation Authorities; and 

• Regular internal communication with all levels of management and staff within FJR Private Flight.



MOBILITY  

% of traveling 

Domestic and overseas travel may be required (<10%).



LANGUAGE SKILLS

• Fluency in English (speaking, reading, & writing).

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Dear Hiring Manager,

I am excited to submit my application for the position of Continuing Airworthiness Manager (CAM) with your esteemed organization. As an experienced aviation professional with a comprehensive knowledge of CAR M, I am confident that I can effectively contribute to the development, implementation, and maintenance of CAMO, systems and plans, to meet GCAA objectives of being best-in-class and in compliance with all applicable regulatory and customer requirements.

Throughout my career, I have built a strong track record of reliability assessment, forecasting planned maintenance work, and coordinating with maintenance providers to ensure airworthiness of aircraft as per CAR M. Additionally, I have a vast experience in AD review and embodiment, service bulletin evaluation, and coordinating test flights. My experience in coordinating with GCAA during aircraft deregistration process will also support me in the position. 

Furthermore, I possess excellent skills in work planning and scheduling, budgetary control, inventory control, subcontract coordination, planning of safety-critical tasks, and coordination with production planning. I am also experienced in monitoring the performance of products in-service, recording service difficulties, and assessing significance. My ability to devise and provide timely rectification action towards airworthiness issues will be valuable while carrying out the responsibilities of the CAM.

My educational background and training courses in aviation engineering have enabled me to provide effective and efficient continuing airworthiness management. I have completed training courses, equivalent to CAR-66, Appendix III Level 1 General Familiarization, and hold an Aircraft Maintenance Engineer’s License (AMEL) without limitations. My advanced IT skills in MS Office, Excel, and other latest manual development software will also come in handy for the role.

I am confident that my strong problem-solving ability, long and short-term strategic planning skills and experience, and judgment will contribute to ensuring the continued airworthiness of the aircraft operated by FJR Private Flight. I look forward to remaining accountable for the continuing airworthiness of the aircraft, maintaining current and updated continuing airworthiness records of aircraft, and corresponding with GCAA and any other relevant authority in all matters relating to the Continuing Airworthiness Management System Procedures and documentation.

Thank you for considering my application. I look forward to discussing how I can contribute to your organization.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: pourriez vous m'informer, pourquoi le TVA dans les sociétés tunisienne,  ne compte pas pour le matériel de transport tourisme comme voiture etc..?

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
En Tunisie, les sociétés ont le droit de déduire la TVA payée sur les biens et les services qu'elles achètent pour les besoins de leur activité économique. Cependant, il est important de noter que les règles de déduction de TVA peuvent varier en fonction de la nature des biens et des services considérés.

En ce qui concerne le matériel de transport touristique, comme les voitures, les règles de déduction de la TVA peuvent être différentes de celles applicables aux autres biens et services. En Tunisie, il existe une disposition fiscale qui prévoit que la TVA payée sur l'achat de véhicules de tourisme utilisés à des fins touristiques ne peut pas être récupérée.

Cette disposition a été mise en place pour encourager le développement du secteur du tourisme en Tunisie, en aidant les agences de voyage et les entreprises de transport touristique à réduire leurs coûts et à offrir des tarifs compétitifs aux clients. Elle vise également à encourager le développement du transport public dans les zones touristiques, en offrant des avantages fiscaux aux entreprises qui investissent dans l'amélioration de leur flotte de transport.

Il est important de noter que cette règle de non-déductibilité de la TVA pour le matériel de transport touristique ne s'applique pas à tous les types d'entreprises en Tunisie, mais uniquement à celles qui exercent des activités liées au tourisme. Les règles de déduction de la TVA peuvent donc varier en fonction du type d'entreprise et des biens et services achetés.
